mentions.note: Riding the broad retail buying wave along with the discounters.
Up 4% on ISM report signaling retail demand strength; new CEO undergoing transition but benefiting from broader retail rally.
Value-oriented retailer; makes things cheaper than competitors; reports tomorrow; on Cramer's list of understandable businesses.
80% of trillion-dollar march came from multiple expansion. Added quality/convenience to price appeal. High-income shoppers increasingly active. MVP thesis play.
Up 8% YTD; high multiples but defensive if slowdown nears; bargain valuation not obvious but quality stable.
Consumer stocks surging alongside financials on peace narrative; highlighted as AI force-multiplier example by OpenAI CFO (merchandising, coding, consumer search).
Defensive staple play; beneficiary of economic stress; represents difficult economy thesis; buy in war/oil environment.
Safe defensive trade; bounced off lows; ready to run; earnings power and stability make this buy for conservative investors seeking portfolio anchor.
Barely up in rally; pathetic showing for retail strength needed to validate broad advance.
Trading 42x FY2027 earnings (near all-time high multiple); -3.4% suggests stock too expensive for consumers; runaway train needs validation.
Incredible run; consolidating terrifically; retail exposure consolidating well; liked in diversified portfolio.
Had run hard pre-war, now experiencing huge reversal alongside Target; understandable consolidation but part of broader retail malaise.
Among greatest companies of era; revamped for all income levels; excellent selection and pricing; terrific earnings expected.
Matched SSS expectations (4.1%) but sky-high valuation; management cited new consumer pressure; 7.2% pullback is rare buying opportunity if consumer stabilizes.
Down 3.9% today, in red YTD on oil/gasoline thesis that's nonsense; 37× earnings expensive but down 26 points from highs feels excessive; use dip to accumulate.

Down 18% from May $135 peak despite improving fundamentals (oil fell from $110 to low $70s, gas from $4.56 to $3.79). Q2 report fine; miss triggered by guidance reiteration vs. raise expectations and softer Q3. Trump price-cut announcement and tariff refund upside (0.5% of sales) not yet baked in. Buying opportunity as consumer headwinds ease.
Rolling back prices in a way that should bring shoppers back despite a soft quarter; a reliable bounce-back name.
Announced Monday price rollbacks specifically naming Frito-Lay chips and Pepsi sodas — likely forced PepsiCo's pricing hand.
